danger_signals.visual_form: Demon sprites with aggressive postures, blood-red coloration, grotesque morphology
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostile threat in level space; proximity requires combat readiness

danger_signals.visual_form: Lava, acid, crushers, spikes rendered as bright contrasting textures against walls/floors
danger_signals.meaning: Non-combat damage sources; environmental threat to player health

danger_signals.visual_form: Cyberdemon (mechanical demon hybrid), Icon of Sin (massive architectural entity) — significantly larger scale than standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Major world-state threat; progression gate requiring sustained engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Glowing anomalies, demonic architecture, swirling vortex textures
danger_signals.meaning: Source of continuous enemy spawning; world under demonic invasion

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating sprites of guns (shotgun, rocket launcher, plasma rifle) with bright, distinct coloration
safety_signals.meaning: Increased combat capability; tactical resource acquisition

safety_signals.visual_form: Medkits, soul spheres (glowing orbs), Megasphere (large radiant sphere)
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration or temporary invulnerability; survival resources

safety_signals.visual_form: Exit switch (textured panel), teleporter pad (distinctive floor marking)
safety_signals.meaning: Progression point; safe transition to next map area

safety_signals.visual_form: Interior rooms, corridors, enclosed chambers with minimal demon density
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary respite area; reduced ambient threat

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red, dark brown, black, sickly green
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pentagram, demonic iconography, skull motifs in architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Imps (red horned sprites), Cacodemons (floating red spheres with teeth), Lost Souls (skull projectiles), Cyberdemons (mechanical demon hybrids), Revenants (skeletal armored demons), Mancubi (obese demon tanks), Arachnotrons (spider-demon hybrids)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell-themed architecture (demonic temples, blood-stained walls), lava pits, demonic banners, corrupted human structures repurposed as demonic temples

faction_indicators.color_identity: Gray, industrial metallic tones, occasional human-scale architecture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military base architecture, research facility remnants, technological infrastructure being overtaken by demonic forces

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Demonic invasion in progress (primary narrative state)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Human installations defaced/corrupted by demonic architecture; mixed human-industrial and hellish aesthetics; demonic symbols overlaid on human structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High demon spawn density; enemies patrol aggressively; multiple simultaneous threats in combat encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Glowing portal textures, swirling energy vortices, architectural distortion around gateway points
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy reinforcements entering level; portal closure/opening triggers major encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Military base textures damaged/bloodstained; demonic creatures inhabiting human structures; barricades and fortifications visibly breached
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No human NPCs present; only hostile demons in human-built spaces; level objectives involve clearing demonic occupation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Minimal enclosed spaces; open level design exposes player to ambient threat; sparse resource availability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant engagement requirements; fewer opportunities for resource gathering between encounters

environmental_storytelling.example: Early levels show human military/industrial design; later levels transition to pure hellish architecture, visually communicating player progression from defended human space into demonic realm
Walls transition from clean military gray to blood-stained, skull-decorated surfaces; gore detail increases; storytelling player descent into hell through visual degradation of human aesthetics
Early levels: sparse enemy placement, readable spaces. Late levels: cramped demonic temples, high enemy density. Visual communication of invasion intensity escalation
Human technology repurposed in demonic context (demonic altars in military bases, hellish statuary in research facilities) communicates faction overlap and invasion success
Icon of Sin final boss positioned as massive architectural entity dominating cityscape; visual communication of existential threat scale

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary or slow patrol; minimal threat posture
Fast lateral movement, aggressive vocalizations, projectile attacks indicate combat engagement
Slow floating approach, circling motion communicates tactical awareness and threat evaluation
Heavy mechanical movement, massive melee range, rocket weapon fire communicates overwhelming force state
HUD coloration shift (red), visual damage feedback, audio distortion communicate critical state
Synchronized movement patterns, multiple simultaneous attacks communicate coordinated threat and player disadvantage in open spaces

notes: Doom II uses visual language primarily through enemy morphology, environmental aesthetics, and architectural contrast to communicate world-state. The game lacks explicit faction dialogue/narrative but relies on visual semiotics: demonic entities are distinctly non-human/grotesque, human spaces are industrial/military, and corruption is shown through architectural hybridization. Danger is signaled through enemy presence, environmental hazard visibility, and scale differentiation (boss monsters are dramatically larger). Safety is implied through resource availability and closed architectural spaces. The progression from human military bases to pure hellish architecture creates implicit narrative about invasion success and player movement into enemy territory.
